item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Possibly relevant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the necro, but it can't be 9, because Theta Prime is between two single digits.

You might be on the nose with 7, though.

EDIT: Oh God—upon further reflection, based on your observation, I think SCP-033 could be the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might be my favorite SCP on the site, and I think the reason why is because of how its anomalousness differs from so many other SCPs. A lot of SCPs are anomalous because they defy the rules of the universe that we observe, but 033 is anomalous because it defies a rule that humanity created.

Say, for example, that θ' is between 5 and 6. 6 is specifically defined as the integer after 5, a definition that we humans came up with, yet θ' still manages to be between them anyway. 033 isn't just anomalous; it's semantically anomalous, refusing to obey a definition made law not by the universe but by us. That's a level of anomalousness that not many other SCPs reach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even for those human cultures that don't even have words for numbers above 3, it's not hard to communicate small numbers to people of those cultures. The concepts of "none" and "only" and "pair" are universal, and with enough effort you can explain just about everything starting with the numbers 0-2.

This is not true. The Pirahã only have words for "smaller quantity" and "larger quantity", not for one or two, and efforts to teach willing tribe members to count failed. This has led to speculation that other languages previously believed to include a word for the number one may have been mistranslated.

Despite such disadvantages, the Pirahã consider their language to be the only correct one. In light of this scip, they may be right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you think θ' has some cool non anomalous properties?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a proof, an equation.
This may seem a pointless distinction, but it is a pivotal difference because I am a math nerd.

A proof is a statement about the subject. If you were very careful, you could write a proof establishing some fact about Theta Prime without actually using the number. So the proof would not trigger the anomaly. A written proof that all swans are white need not actually contain any version of the word "swans".

But we're told 033 is "a series of symbols" the sum of which is theta prime. So, an equation that looks something like A+B+C+D=Theta Prime.

In the framework of math, "=" is just the symbol for "is". The two sides of an equation are literally the same thing.
Like two copies of the same jigsaw puzzle, one assembled and one loose pieces. But both still the same puzzle.

So it would be equally accurate for the article to say the skip is the single-digit number or the string of symbols that amounts to a disassembled version of the same number. It comes to the same difference as writing in pen or pencil, all forms trigger the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think people have overlooked that Theta Prime is not the anomaly. There is no evidence in the article that the number itself is anomalous. It's the proof of its existence that's anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't post here much but I do read a lot of SCPs. This one is my absolute favorite for a lot of reasons. Unlike most of the SCPs listed, this is right on the verge of credible (IN SOME WAYS) and that makes it so much more intriguing. This SCP hovers right on the line between Math and Philosophy, a mathematician will tell you this is an impossibility but a philosopher would say otherwise. You'd think the mathematicians word would be more credible but this is really a matter of perspective.

+ show block
– hide block

Taking everything at face value 033 presents a bunch of different and equally alarming possibilities. The most basic and obvious possibility is our entire numeric system is wrong, and true math is incomprehensible to humans. Similar to how our brain makes thousands of leaps of judgement based on prior information to save computing power, our math is a simplified thing that only partially works and doesn't encompass the full system. Like looking at shadows on a cave wall and thinking the shadows are the real objects. Whereas the inferior math we work allows us to create objects and utilize the basic rules of the universe this "higher math" would more likely directly alter reality and resemble magic or programming the universe directly. Which of course brings about simulation theory.

Taking this in an entirely different direction, someone else posited that this was a residual number from another universe where math functions differently. This is also an amazing theory because in the SCP universe it's been shown that the platonic ideal of concepts as idealized objects is possible. Someone found an idealized physical representation of a concept not native to our universe and by expressing it causes very localized degradation of reality where expressed. This is really interesting because as someone else pointed out this is the best definition of forbidden knowledge. No one is forbidding you, it's just knowledge that harms you to have and has no benefit and you should have no way of learning. By attempting to apply a concept foreign to our universe to it's physical parameters it just clashes with the math of our universe. If this is the case nuclear fission should be possible if you are unlucky depending on how the paper degraded because even the atomic structures would differ.
edit- or the symbol itself is a platonic ideal from another universe expressed in that one.

Alternatively, 033 could be part of a mathematical system we simply haven't fully discovered or implemented yet. Referring back the Godel's incompleteness theorem, no mathematical system can be all encompassing, correct, and provable at the same time. This could be part of a series of math's not used or usable in the conventional sense. While our math is correct for what we use it for, maybe this hints at problems we could never solve without understanding this.

But I have a chicken and egg question regarding 033. What came first, our discovery of the mathematic principle underlining 033 or the symbol representing it? This matters a lot.

The theory I ascribe to though is that 033 is either a memetic or cognitohazardous effect not connected to math at all with minor reality warping properties. How do you write down the number that 033 represents? Did we find it in a cave somewhere? My guess is, we made up a symbol to represent this integer (IF IT WAS DISCOVERED THAT WAY). Why would this made up symbol have any effect on reality or the structure of objects it's on? Why would reality correlate our place holder symbol with this fundamentally incompatible math and cause such a reaction?

If we made the symbol for 033 it wouldn't do anything. The only place 033 would really be existing is in our brains. If I type the word "car" here, there is no car. The car only exists in your mind, connecting these letters and making them into a concept you can correlate. Even if someone sat down and worked out a giant formula proving the existence of 033, after the "=" would be a symbol a man created to represent the concept of that number. So if we made the symbol it should have no effect on reality.

On the flip side, if we FOUND the symbol why do we assume it has anything to do with math? Unless we found it, like inscribed on a stone tablet with the proof of formula right there as well. But even then, this would absolutely not be trustworthy. There are tons of cognitohazardous reality warping items in SCP custody. A ring where any lie told is indistinguishable from truth to everyone who hears it (even if they KNOW it's a lie), a white board that makes anything written on it a viral meme that becomes reality to anyone it spreads to etc. I don't think the foundation could ever verify if this was mathematical in nature or not because the proof itself could be the cognitohazard that convinces you it's real.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

I think 033 is just a memetic cognitohazardous conceptual disease with mild reality warping capabilities. It tries to spread itself as a mathematic concept, inserting itself into peoples minds to be spread but fails spectacularly to be incorporated into math because it's fundamentally not. It would be like trying to add your child's hand drawing to a mathematical equation, it just makes no sense to even try but because of how 033 effects your mind you can't understand that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something that's not been mentioned in any of the comments so far… the entry for SCP-033 uses 0,1,2,3,4,5,6 and 8… but not 7 or 9. Theta prime is exotic to the authors of this document, but that doesn't prove it's exotic to us. Theta prime could be 7.

-7 returning to a universe that somehow 'lost' it
-7 intruding on a universe that wasn't meant to have it
-Amnestic effects making 7 seem like an exotic, undiscovered number in a world where it's the norm.

Theta prime could also be 9, (or A, B, C, etc) if this article is from a universe accustomed to a higher-base allowing it to fit the 'between single digits' constraint. But I find the idea that this skip could be trying to introduce what we see as mundane to a situations we would find exotic much more interesting than that.

It's especially interesting if post SCP-033 math really is the 'right' math, and technically the world *should* be moving toward it, but the short term cost of giving up all existing theory and possibly all computer systems, to start from scratch is extraordinarily high, and may be higher than any possible long term gain. If Relativity had been an all or nothing choice that forced an immediate, worldwide, cold turkey end to Newtonian calculations in all situations no matter how informal, we probably never would have found a way to afford the changeover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ow my head.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps the most interesting thing about the readers' response to this item is that the majority of readers - responders, at least - seem to be attempting to fit it into the existing ruleset of our numerical construct. They insinuate that 'if θ' belongs between 3 and 4, then 1θ' belongs between 13 and 14'. However, at least from my reading of it, that's not the case at all. We have this single result, proven by SCP-033 as belonging between x and y single-digit numbers. No such equation exists with a solution that belongs between xx and yy double-digit numbers. There's no deviation from our base-10 counting system, there's simply an anomalous construct that exists within the first 10 numbers on the line.

To the author: you've done a spectacular job of creating something so deviant from our understanding of reality that a large subset of readers simply can't wrap their head around it. Good job, sir (or ma'am or otherwise).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Math lover that I am, I love this idea.

item.title: Re: +400
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I actually wrote it after a conversation with a mathematician friend of mine.
As a mathematician, I find this article really funny and well-written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried dividing this by zero.
The paper exploded.

Anyways yeah this is an old article but I might as well say I really enjoy the concept of it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Object class: E U C L I D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: love it when scps make me really think. don't even really understand whats going on here cause its something thats meant to be impossible to Really understand, and I love it

item.title: An analogy that might help some people understand this
item.content:encoded: A brick wall is solid as a brick; but what happens to it if we add one contagious sand brick? It starts falling apart in spite of it still being made of bricks. The Universe is built of math bricks, Theta Prime is a type of math brick that can't hold the current "shape" of the Universe; when you try to add Theta Prime to anything in the Universe, it infects stuff around it adding the same type of brick to the composition of stuff around it; things that are more mathematical like shapes closer to geometrically perfect, and computing devices, depend on exact math on a more fundamental level and so suffer the effect more strongly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another way that this can work:

Somewhere in some other constructed universe, theta prime is among the the number system used there. Obviously its incompatible with our number system, and detrimental effect kicks in shall this interaction occurs.
However it happened anyways, so yeah.

item.title: Scientific query
item.content:encoded: So, seeing how if you write this on a normal piece of paper the destruction process appears to result in the paper becoming "water logged" and/or becoming an unrecognisable pulp, what would happen if you were to tattoo this onto a human or a similarly 'alive' being?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some more necro posting here (not as extreme) but that wholly depends on what definition you're using for the integers. Specifically, of the two definitions I know of 1+1=2 is a definition in one of them. Specifically this is a definition in the system by which the integers are the unique infinite cyclic group.

However the system most often used is that of peanno arithmetic (technically this gives the natural numbers not the integers but whatever). In this, the nth natural number is defined to be the nth successor of 0 (or 1 if you prefer) and addition and multiplication are defined recursively. In this system it does require proof that 1+1=2; that is, the first successor of zero plus the first successor of zero is the second successor of zero.

That said it absolutely does not take 8 pages to prove this, so I'm assuming Penrose is using some other system. Or maybe he was constructing a full formal proof with no logical assumptions in which case he's lucky it didn't end up being 100 pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to clear a misconception that I feel like a lot of people are having. I keep seeing "that just turns it into Base-11" or "It's impossible to miss a number as we are the ones who define it". I would just like to say that this is exactly why it's anomalous, let me explain

SCP-033 doesn't change the fact that we are in Base 10. If we were counting all the fingers(including thumb) on our left hand we would count like this: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. If we included SCP-033 it would be: 1, 2, θ', 3, 4, 5.

We do not end up with more than five fingers nor do we stop counting at 4, it simply inserts itself into our counting. θ' is basically stating that there are still only 10 numbers inside Base-10 just with the inclusion of θ'; this is why the doctor stated that it is like trying to force a book inside a bookcase that is already full.

This also explains the physical malady where it destroys constructs that it is contained upon(paper and mechanical). Geometrical shapes would also be forced to include the dimensions of θ' meaning that an extra dimension that is an impossibility to exist and not to exist would display some reality destroying capabilities.

In my own head canon, I would believe that θ' is a number whose information has a considerably lower amount of Humes and causes any information(including properties of matter) to be affected by this in an extreme way. Of course as the author stated, this can be taken in any way you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty interesting SCP, and as of yet, definitely one of my favorites. Math, while not being one of my strongest subjects, is something I'm quite fond of, and I've always thought of situations like this where an extra number is added to our typical ones. The idea of it completely corrupting whatever system it's introduced to is a nice touch, too.
I also find it kind of funny that a lot of these comments say that this "isn't possible" for various logical reasons, because that's the point. It can't logically exist in any current systems.
GuessWho's idea is a nice one, with it changing everything in it's area to one where it logically exists, which would explain the papers, since it wouldn't be able to change properly within the given time, causing it to not exist at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WRITE A TEST LOG GAWD DAMN

cause that would be a great way to revive 033

i wanna know what happens to computers that attempt to make mathematical calculations using 033 in the integer sequence!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's a concept that destroys systems that think about it.

This is the core of 033 yet all he has is one log of paper disolving and a whole lot of scary warnings about using a wide range of materials. That implies a larger test log. He needs to write a larger test log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah bra, that's just cause the statue moves through quantum tunneling and it warps reality to do so. Their tunneling distance is limited by the presence of an observer. If the last remaining observer has their attention averted for a time, 173's position becomes unknown, thus it is granted enough momentum for being at a new position when interacting with an observer (such as the observer bringing their gaze to where 173 happens to be given the momentum it had. In close quarters, 173 is able to quickly get behind what it recognizes as observers and finish them off before they can observer 173. It is possible that doing this multiple times in a short period may empower 173's abilities…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is precisely why i decided to downvote this scp, the mathematicians have the doom and gloom of people with art degrees who can imagine storylines but don't notice the breadth of conceptual knowledge we have worked with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my headcanon, this thing is the remnant of an alternate universe. In this universe, maths works differently, and there is another number between █ and █. It relies on completely different laws of physics, mathematics and logic. 033 is the imprint that this universe has left on our own. It attempts to restructure the area around it to fit itself, and in doing so causes the breakdown of conventional geometry.
It is, essentially, a tiny fragment of an alien dimension in which it can exist plausibly, and has somehow been imprinted on/trapped in our universe, where it can't. The concept doesn't have to work here, because it works there.

Considering we have an AO that's a word meaning "the opposite of a sieve", it's not too much of a stretch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very very necro posting here, but just a few things to point out.
Firstly, I searched for this Penrose online, but I found nothing about a proof of one plus one is two.
Secondly, the said proof isn't valid in the first place. As in, there is no need to prove it, as 2 IS DEFINED to be 1+1. We do not prove a definition.

This can counter what PlasmaFox had said: we use the number as it is because we defined them to be that way, not the other way round.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a neat explanation, but given the number in question causes the surface it's printed on to decay, I don't think that's what the author had in mind when he wrote this.

And yeah, it still doesn't explain why we skip a number when counting objects, in a system we invented for counting objects. People keep explaining this as an abstract mathematical proof, and as an example of how hard math is weird. But this doesn't deal with hard math. It's about an integer between a one digit number and a one digit number. Suspension of disbelief only functions up to a certain point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of these math majors posting here, and the only thing this SCP is bringing to mind for me is "derf" from iCarly. The new number between 4 and 5 that the Math Council put in.

…iCarly. I'm an idiot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure I understand this objection. A plain old scalene triangle fulfills this condition nicely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually a thing in the real world. Number theory is only a small piece of math, for starters; there are forms of math that don't even use numbers as we normally think of them. The more abstract fields of geometry and graph theory only use numbers as useful way to keep track of coordinates and vectors, while Knot based mathematics uses binary overlaps of a loop to represent values. (Knot theory is trippy, by the way. It's like seeing how an alien culture might conceive of math.) The radian system bases its integers off of fractions and multiples of ℼ for better compatibility with polar equations, and Discrete mathematics is the study of things like numbering systems that have non-sequential values- you could theoretically construct a functioning system of math that skipped the number three, for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, you can simulate and explore a concept like this fairly easily:

Let W, X, and Y be consecutive whole numbers such that W < X < Y

  • X + 1 $≜$1 Y
  • X - 1 $≜$ W
  • W - X $≜$ 1
  • X - Y $≜$ 1

X is SCP-033, and this is the problem with it:

If a mathematical construct implies that W - Y $≜$ 1 $⇒$ either:

  • the entire construct is invalid.
  • The construct is a representation of a non-standard system not completely compatible with normal math.
  • or unusual circumstances have created a scenario which exposes a programming flaw (See crashing Garry's Mod by overloading the physics package with fine detail or making something divide by zero)

The anomaly is not the missing number. The anomaly is that we think W - Y = 1, when it clearly does not.

Footnotes
1. $≜$: "must equal by basic definition"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was convinced to downvote this entry upon reading "no side's length should be equal to any other," as that is never a condition that would exist in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a fan of that interpretation as well. There's even a way to justify the apparent mathematics — you can "prove" anything you want to if you sneak a divide-by-zero into the math, and if the actual anomalous phenomena keeps people from realizing that it's there, then something like "five minus four equals two, therefore Theta Prime" is trivial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this has been mentioned before, but would it be possible to create a new form of mathematics that integrates 033? And if so, what would be the potential repercussions of that?

It's the hallmark of science – if some new data disproves your theory, you throw the theory out and come up with a new one. Only in this case, it's…all of mathematics, but the Foundation has lots of smart people working for it, right? ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have always been happy my 033 has endured to incite debate and discussion for so long. This is a thoughtful analysis, and elements of it are actually eerily close to my original motivations for creating this little thought experiment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, glad to hear it. =)

I do sympathize with a lot of the negative comments. This is obviously more "incoherent" than most other SCPs; if this thing ever existed, it wouldn't just be anomalous, it would make reality incomprehensible. That makes it impossible to fit this into a canon that has any kind of internal consistency. Also, this thing apparently cares about things like right angles of the paper it is written on; to put it simply, paper is only perceived as having a perfect right angle by humans, but physically the angle is never perfect. So if you take the skip even a little seriously, it has to be a memetic phenomenon, because the physical parts make no sense.

That said, this is one of the old-school SCPs that wasn't supposed to be scientifically perfect, but which was supposed to be a good work of philosophical/speculative flash fiction. And from that perspective, it does a damn good job. In my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice comment. You've gotten me to like this skip a lot more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm versed enough in math and philosophy of mathematics to know that this is ridiculous, which is, in a sense, why I like this one. No matter how much of the human experience you consider to be socially constructed, small integers are as universal as it gets. Even for an alien civilization, it's hard to imagine how they could be technologically advanced without being able to count to 10. Even for those human cultures that don't even have words for numbers above 3, it's not hard to communicate small numbers to people of those cultures. The concepts of "none" and "only" and "pair" are universal, and with enough effort you can explain just about everything starting with the numbers 0-2.

This is one of the lowest-number SCPs, and also the one that really sets the highest bar for how anomalous things can be. There's none of this business like SCP-1184 or SCP-2330, where the reader gets to pity people who are unable to see "obvious" truths. (Not that those are bad SCPs at all, just that they are written from the perspective of the ostensibly unaffected.) In contrast, SCP-033 is a patently ridiculous, yet apparently real, mathematical entity.

If you take the file as more-or-less being the literal truth, it implies that something is deeply wrong with reality, something that not only messes with human minds, but also with the nature of reality itself (e.g. broken computers, transmuted paper). On the other hand, if you are more technically minded, and you think of it like you would something in the real world, it pretty much has to be memetic.

But then you have an ambiguity. Is it the case that SCP-033 anomalously convinces people of the existence of a false integer, which doesn't really exist? Or is Theta Prime a real integer that has been hidden throughout human history, with SCP-033 being an anomalous counter-meme that restores knowledge of that integer? Or is mathematics far less objective than we thought, to such an extent where some universes involve mathematics involving Theta Prime, and some (like ours) without it, and SCP-033 is a troublesome gate between them?

In reality, no one really does mathematics with a "different" set of integers, because the integers are defined by their order. We know that there is no integer between 4 and 5, because 5 is defined as the integer immediately after 4, whatever that is. However, we have conflicting ideas about what axioms are fundamental in mathematics, and this (along with Tarski's undefinability theorem) means that there's not a universal conception of mathematical truth. E.g. some people consider the axiom of choice to be obvious, and a smaller set consider the axiom of decidability to be obvious, or at least reasonable. However, these two are in direct conflict with each other; either one is wrong, or both are wrong, or we have to accept a less strict conception of mathematical truth.

SCP-033 is a simple window into an ongoing mathematical and philosophical issue: to what extent, and on what basis, should modern mathematics be considered objectively correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes a lot more sense than the rest of the theories in the discussion thread. It's hard to word why this is also plausible for the Foundation to think the end anomaly is Theta-Prime, rather than it being █ (or, well, 5 in that example), but I feel like it could be plausible. Certainly, it seems like the anomaly is ambiguous enough that they only theorize it to be Theta-Prime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The more I think of it, the more I believe that this is actually a special anomalous combination of mathematical symbols that causes magic, like a set of runes drawn in chalk and used in magic rituals. The strange requirement for "033-Safe dimensions", the weird leaping that happens for no clear reason, and just how plain invalid the whole "extra integer" is all point to this NOT being a mathematical concept.

My idea is that this is actually an anomalous combination of mathematical symbols, with properties including breaking down everything it is stored on (having 033-Safe dimensions and being on hand-crafted vellum reduces the effectiveness), leaping between different surfaces, and making people believe that its sum is an unknown integer between 4 and 6, when in reality its sum is 5.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like it changes reality around it so that it becomes a real thing in that area, and then everything breaks down because an extra number means that all the basic equations in physics have to be different for it to be there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to dispute your point, but what about a train that is also a horse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And that is why the laws of physics start to break down when you try to input it into anything more orderly; it doesn't fit with "our" reality, the square peg destroys the round hole if you try to force it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like math. I have a pretty good grasp of number theory, set theory, etc.

But this, see, it's one thing for somebody to say "It's just fiction, anything can happen because it's anomalous" but that doesn't work here.

It's just not a valid concept. It can't happen. It's like saying the foundation discovered a horse that is a car. Not like a car, superficially similar to a car, a car made of horse-matter, a regular horse, that is a car. See the disconnect? It's a horse and a horse is just not a car and no amount of magic hand waving, attempts at subverting logic or saying it is so because it is anomalous can make it so. An object being a horse, and an object being a car, are mutually exclusive.

This is not an integer between 4 and 5. Even in a fantasy realm, this is just not a thing that can happen. If it did then pretty much by definition it's not actually part of the system you're claiming it's part of. Putting something between 4 and 5 by DEFINITION of our system of math means it is not an integer. Maybe it's a new class of number, something which mimics the properties of integers for all known tests, but calling it an integer is like calling a horse a car.

item.title: Re: Oops I just accidentally the article.
item.content:encoded:
That circle thing with a line through it is the Greek letter Theta. The apostrophe indicates that it's "Prime."

Theta Prime. As in, SCP-033.

It's a dumb math joke.

item.title: Re: Oops I just accidentally the article.
item.content:encoded: I don't understand this joke.

item.title: Oops I just accidentally the article.
item.content:encoded:
θ′

Am I funny yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the problem with SCP-033 is that the author intended to show that SCP-033 causes physical breakdown, while most of us are just thinking about conceptual mathematical breakdown.

As the author, I could explain the whole of my intent, and may do so in that "Leak" thread wherein such things are outlined at some point. But for now, as I have for many years, I am happy to watch as this discussion continues.

Filtering out my intent is likely going to be less fun than simply joining the long-standing (and at times vitriolic) debate on this entry, but I appreciate the interest. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that 'gird' was the first thing I thought of when I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of this being evidence for the simulation hypothesis is lovely! I'm just visualizing a cosmic, metaphysical programmer -possibly just a grad-student working up its project for a complex simulation course finding out that near the beginning, it forgot to code an integer. The solution is simple (well, easier than starting over) - just lower every subsequent integer by one across the entire project, right down to the OS and language. But it missed a single solitary occurrence of the integer…

Or, for a different fun perspective: a lot of people have downvoted and/or complained because they feel the inclusion of theta-prime is a terrible mistake, threatening the internal logic and consistency of the SCP universe. This is because, from our perspective, we know that universe to be a simulation (actually, with all of our headcanons, a vast number of broadly similar simulations), and because we cannot process the inclusion of theta-prime, it messes with the simulations.

Which now reminds me that Voct needs to continue the Metafiction tale!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the problem with SCP-033 is that the author intended to show that SCP-033 causes physical breakdown, while most of us are just thinking about conceptual mathematical breakdown.

TBH, I don't think that a mathematical concept causing physical breakdown makes sense at all. The description of the article just further pushes it at the direction of conceptual breakdown while only showing us that it causes physical breakdown.

Maybe SCP-033 isn't even a mathematical concept. It's just a special combination of mathematical symbols that causes physical breakdown.

item.title: How about non-permament and analog forms of storage?
item.content:encoded:
What would happen if someone stored SCP-033 in an analog magnetic tape, not as binary data, but as current fluctuations that describe the motion required to write it? Record it with a non-electronic autopen? And how about using those laser projectors that bounce a laser on a moving mirror to trace pictures? And what if someone writes it on a transparency and place it on one of those oldschool projectors? (the transparency itself would probably suffer the fate of regular paper, but what about the image on the screen?)

And what if someone draws it on the sand or some other powdery substance? What about writing with said powdery substance? And mixing different substances?

And how about those sculptures that you need to look from a very specific perspective to see a picture, and just look like random chunks spread in random positions and orientations from all other angles?

And what if someone converts an image of it into a spectrogram and records it on a vinyl disc? Would it matter if there was lots of noise present?

edit: And how about storing it with visual cryptography?

item.title: A hypothesis on the "nature" of 033
item.content:encoded:
There are a few comments here where this would be an appropriate reply, but I don't wanna post it more than once, so I'm making a new thread instead.

Based on the description, I have the feeling 033 might be a bit like Tupper's self-referential formula, but actually more like a quine program; but a bit more viral, with it's robustness and resilience to being transfered to different mediums reminding me of the likes of Stuxnet; but probably using techniques analog to this hack (notice how the reality of the game starts deteriorating right when the more intense part starts; the paper being destroyed reminds me of that).

SCP-033 might be providing strong evidence towards the Simulation Hypothesis

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The brain is probably too irregular for it to do it's thing on it.

I'm not sure about the second question though; perhaps it will speed up the medium's destruction due to the imperfection?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something along the lines of 055, I reckon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what SCP-00Θ´ would be.

item.content:encoded:
Visual representation of theta prime for those curious
